items.title: Project group shows truncated branch name
items.precondition: App running; at least one git-repo project in the sidebar.
steps: 1. Open DevEasy and look at the 'Sessions by project' sidebar.
2. Find a project whose repo is on a branch (e.g. 'main').
items.expected: A line under the project name shows a branch icon and the branch name; an overly long branch is truncated with an ellipsis, not wrapped.

items.title: New / modified / deleted counts match git status
items.precondition: A project repo with at least one untracked, one modified, and one deleted file.
steps: 1. In a terminal, run `git status --porcelain=v1` in that repo and tally untracked/added, modified, deleted.
items.expected: Green +N equals new/untracked files, amber ~N equals modified, red -N equals deleted; each file counted once.

items.title: Clean repo shows 'clean'
items.precondition: A project repo with a clean working tree.
steps: 1. Ensure the repo has no changes (`git status` shows clean).
2. View that project in the sidebar.
items.expected: The git line shows the branch and the word 'clean', with no count badges.
